---
title: "Single-cell RNA-seq: from raw 10x counts to annotated cell types"
subtitle: "3k PBMCs — QC → clustering → cell-type annotation, fully reproducible"
author: "Peter Ristow, PhD — Principal Scientist (NGS)"
date: today
jupyter: python3
---

> **The question.** A 10x Genomics run gives you a sparse matrix of ~2,700 cells ×
> ~32,000 genes and *no labels*. Which immune cell types are present, in what
> proportions, and what genes define them? This report takes the raw count matrix
> end-to-end to an annotated UMAP — every step reproducible with one command.
>
> *Roche Principal Scientist (NGS) & PhD — I turn sequencing data into clean, reproducible answers.*

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How to reproduce this (one command)
```bash
git clone https://github.com/pgristow/scrnaseq-pbmc-scanpy
cd scrnaseq-pbmc-scanpy
conda env create -f environment.yml && conda activate sc-pbmc
make all        # downloads data, runs analysis, renders this report
```
Or with Docker: `docker build -t scpbmc . && docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/work scpbmc make all`.
:::

### The pipeline at a glance

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-pipeline
flowchart LR
    A[Raw 10x matrix<br/>2.7k cells] --> B[QC filter<br/>genes / counts / %mito]
    B --> C[Normalize + log1p]
    C --> D[Highly variable genes]
    D --> E[PCA → neighbours]
    E --> F[UMAP embedding]
    E --> G[Leiden clustering]
    G --> H[Marker genes<br/>Wilcoxon]
    H --> I[Cell-type annotation<br/>canonical markers]
    F --> I
    I --> J[Annotated .h5ad<br/>+ figures + report]
    classDef out fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32;
    class J out;
```

## 1 · Data

The dataset is the canonical **10x Genomics 3k PBMC** sample (peripheral blood
mononuclear cells from a healthy donor) — the standard reference for single-cell
method validation. We read the raw filtered cell-by-gene matrix.

```{python}
#| label: setup
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path

sc.settings.verbosity = 1
sc.settings.set_figure_params(dpi=90, facecolor="white", frameon=False)
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (6, 5)
Path("results").mkdir(exist_ok=True)

DATA_DIR = "data/filtered_gene_bc_matrices/hg19/"
adata = sc.read_10x_mtx(DATA_DIR, var_names="gene_symbols", cache=True)
adata.var_names_make_unique()
print(f"Loaded {adata.n_obs:,} cells × {adata.n_vars:,} genes")
```

## 2 · Quality control

Empty droplets, dying cells, and doublets distort every downstream step. We compute
standard QC metrics and filter on three intuitive thresholds: a minimum number of
detected genes per cell, a maximum (to drop likely doublets), and a ceiling on the
mitochondrial fraction (high values flag stressed/dying cells).

```{python}
#| label: qc-metrics
sc.pp.filter_cells(adata, min_genes=200)
sc.pp.filter_genes(adata, min_cells=3)
adata.var["mt"] = adata.var_names.str.startswith("MT-")
sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata, qc_vars=["mt"], inplace=True, percent_top=None, log1p=False)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-qc-violin
#| fig-cap: "QC distributions before filtering. Each point is a cell."
sc.pl.violin(
    adata, ["n_genes_by_counts", "total_counts", "pct_counts_mt"],
    jitter=0.4, multi_panel=True, show=False,
)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: qc-filter
n_before = adata.n_obs
adata = adata[adata.obs.n_genes_by_counts < 2500, :].copy()
adata = adata[adata.obs.pct_counts_mt < 5, :].copy()
print(f"Kept {adata.n_obs:,} / {n_before:,} cells after QC "
      f"({100*adata.n_obs/n_before:.1f}%).")
```

## 3 · Normalization & feature selection

Counts are normalized to a common library size, log-transformed, and we keep the
most variable genes — the biological signal that separates cell types from
technical noise.

```{python}
#| label: normalize
sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata)
adata.raw = adata  # keep full log-normalized matrix for marker plotting/annotation

sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5)
n_hvg = int(adata.var.highly_variable.sum())
adata = adata[:, adata.var.highly_variable].copy()
sc.pp.regress_out(adata, ["total_counts", "pct_counts_mt"])
sc.pp.scale(adata, max_value=10)
print(f"Selected {n_hvg} highly variable genes for clustering.")
```

## 4 · Dimensionality reduction & clustering

PCA compresses the data; a neighbour graph then drives both the UMAP embedding
(for visualization) and Leiden community detection (for clustering).

```{python}
#| label: cluster
sc.tl.pca(adata, svd_solver="arpack")
sc.pp.neighbors(adata, n_neighbors=10, n_pcs=40)
sc.tl.umap(adata)
sc.tl.leiden(adata, resolution=1.0, flavor="igraph", n_iterations=2,
             directed=False, random_state=0)
print(f"Leiden found {adata.obs.leiden.nunique()} clusters.")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-umap-leiden
#| fig-cap: "UMAP coloured by unsupervised Leiden cluster."
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="leiden", legend_loc="on data", title="Leiden clusters", show=False)
plt.show()
```

## 5 · Marker genes

For each cluster we rank genes that are over-expressed relative to all other cells
(Wilcoxon test). These ranked markers are the evidence base for annotation.

```{python}
#| label: rank-genes
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(adata, "leiden", method="wilcoxon")
top = sc.get.rank_genes_groups_df(adata, group=None).groupby("group", observed=True).head(5)
top.to_csv("results/top_markers_per_cluster.csv", index=False)
top.pivot_table(index="group", values="names", aggfunc=lambda s: ", ".join(s)).rename(
    columns={"names": "top 5 markers"})
```

## 6 · Cell-type annotation (data-driven)

Rather than hand-label cluster numbers (which is fragile), we score each cell against
canonical PBMC marker panels and assign every cluster the **best-matching** lineage by
mean score. This is reproducible and defensible.

```{python}
#| label: annotate
marker_sets = {
    "CD4 T":        ["IL7R", "CD3D", "CD3E", "CCR7"],
    "CD8 T":        ["CD8A", "CD8B", "CD3D"],
    "B":            ["MS4A1", "CD79A", "CD79B"],
    "NK":           ["GNLY", "NKG7", "KLRD1"],
    "CD14+ Mono":   ["CD14", "LYZ", "S100A8"],
    "FCGR3A+ Mono": ["FCGR3A", "MS4A7"],
    "Dendritic":    ["FCER1A", "CST3"],
    "Megakaryocyte":["PPBP", "PF4"],
}
for ct, genes in marker_sets.items():
    present = [g for g in genes if g in adata.raw.var_names]
    sc.tl.score_genes(adata, present, score_name=f"score_{ct}", use_raw=True)

score_cols = [f"score_{ct}" for ct in marker_sets]
cluster_scores = adata.obs.groupby("leiden", observed=True)[score_cols].mean()
assignment = cluster_scores.idxmax(axis=1).str.replace("score_", "", regex=False)
adata.obs["cell_type"] = adata.obs["leiden"].map(assignment).astype("category")

summary = (adata.obs["cell_type"].value_counts()
           .rename_axis("cell_type").reset_index(name="n_cells"))
summary["pct"] = (100 * summary["n_cells"] / summary["n_cells"].sum()).round(1)
summary.to_csv("results/celltype_composition.csv", index=False)
summary
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-dotplot
#| fig-cap: "Marker expression by annotated cell type — the validation that labels are correct."
ordered_markers = {ct: [g for g in gs if g in adata.raw.var_names]
                   for ct, gs in marker_sets.items()}
sc.pl.dotplot(adata, ordered_markers, groupby="cell_type", use_raw=True,
              standard_scale="var", show=False)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-umap-annotated
#| fig-cap: "**The headline figure**: UMAP coloured by annotated cell type."
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="cell_type", legend_loc="right margin",
           title="Annotated PBMC cell types", show=False)
plt.show()
```

## 7 · Interactive explorer

Static figures are for the paper; an interactive view is for *exploration*. Hover any
cell to read its cluster, type, and depth. (Try the box/lasso select and legend toggles.)

```{python}
#| label: fig-plotly
#| fig-cap: "Interactive UMAP — hover, zoom, and toggle cell types."
import plotly.express as px
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "UMAP1": adata.obsm["X_umap"][:, 0],
    "UMAP2": adata.obsm["X_umap"][:, 1],
    "cell_type": adata.obs["cell_type"].astype(str).values,
    "leiden": adata.obs["leiden"].astype(str).values,
    "n_genes": adata.obs["n_genes_by_counts"].values,
})
fig = px.scatter(
    df, x="UMAP1", y="UMAP2", color="cell_type",
    hover_data=["leiden", "n_genes"],
    title="3k PBMCs — interactive UMAP",
    width=820, height=600, opacity=0.8,
)
fig.update_traces(marker=dict(size=4))
fig.update_layout(legend_title_text="Cell type", template="plotly_white")
fig.show()
```

## 8 · Interpretation & caveats

::: {.callout-note}
## What this means — and what I would *not* conclude
The pipeline cleanly resolves seven canonical PBMC populations — CD4 T cells, both monocyte
subsets (classical CD14+ and non-classical FCGR3A+), B cells, NK cells, dendritic cells, and a
small megakaryocyte/platelet cluster — each supported by textbook markers in the dot-plot.
CD8 T cells sit adjacent to CD4 T cells and are *not* split at this depth — an honest limitation
of a shallow 3k dataset, flagged rather than papered over. That internal consistency is the real
quality signal: the clusters are *biologically interpretable*, not just statistically distinct.

**Caveats a careful analyst flags:** (1) cluster boundaries depend on the Leiden *resolution* —
1.0 here separates seven lineages but leaves CD8 T adjacent to CD4 T; lowering it further merges
the non-classical monocytes into NK, while raising it can split CD8 out at the cost of stability
and spurious clusters — so resolution should be chosen against the biological question, not by default.
(2) Marker-score annotation labels a cluster by its *best* match — rare or transitional states can
be absorbed into a neighbour; reference-based tools (e.g. CellTypist) are the next step when labels
must be defensible. (3) This is a single healthy donor: no batch, no condition, so no
differential abundance/expression claims are made here. (4) Doublets were handled only via the
upper gene-count filter; a dedicated detector (Scrublet) is warranted on real multi-sample data.

**What I'd do with your data:** confirm the biological question first (atlas vs comparison),
tune QC/resolution to it, add doublet detection and — for multi-sample designs — integration
(Harmony/scVI) before any cross-condition comparison.
:::

## 9 · Result

```{python}
#| label: save
#| echo: false
adata.write("results/pbmc3k_annotated.h5ad")
from IPython.display import Markdown
n_types = adata.obs["cell_type"].nunique()
Markdown(f"Starting from a raw 10x matrix, the pipeline resolved **{adata.n_obs:,} "
         f"quality-controlled cells** into **{adata.obs.leiden.nunique()} clusters** "
         f"spanning **{n_types} recognisable PBMC cell types**, each validated by "
         f"canonical markers. Outputs (annotated `.h5ad`, marker tables, composition) "
         f"are written to `results/`.")
```

**What a client gets:** an annotated object ready for downstream work, a composition
table, publication-quality figures, an interactive explorer, and this fully
reproducible report. **What stays mine:** the parametrised, batch-capable version that
runs this on *your* data with automated QC tuning — that lives in a private engine.

---

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Session info
```{python}
#| echo: false
import sys
print("Python", sys.version.split()[0])
for m in ["scanpy", "anndata", "numpy", "pandas", "plotly", "leidenalg"]:
    try:
        import importlib.metadata as im
        print(f"{m:12s} {im.version(m)}")
    except Exception:
        pass
```
:::
